characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: For reasons unknown, Jackie calls Adam "Bobble". Drowning My Sorrows: She drinks gin and tonic when she's depressed. Jewish Mother: Infrequent, but it's there. In "The Girlfriend", Adam and Jonny reveal they both have girlfriends and she showers them with love and praise, much to their annoyance. Obliquely Obfuscated Occupation: We never learn what her job is, or if she has one. Sitcom Character Archetypes: The Square. She's a fairly normal suburban mum who often playsOnly Sane Manto the male members of her family.

characters.name: Jackie Goodman
characters.description: Affectionate Nickname: For reasons unknown, Jackie calls Adam "Bobble". Drowning My Sorrows: She drinks gin and tonic when she's depressed. Jewish Mother: Infrequent, but it's there. In "The Girlfriend", Adam and Jonny reveal they both have girlfriends and she showers them with love and praise, much to their annoyance. Obliquely Obfuscated Occupation: We never learn what her job is, or if she has one. Sitcom Character Archetypes: The Square. She's a fairly normal suburban mum who often playsOnly Sane Manto the male members of her family.

characters.name: Martin Goodman
characters.description: All Jews Are Cheapskates: Martin eats out of the bin, spoons unused ketchup back into the bottle and saves back-copies of New Scientist Magazine on the grounds that they'll be worth something someday as "collectibles". Even his shed is filled with junk he's kept over the years. Amazingly Embarrassing Parents: Martin is absolutely shameless when it comes to his habits and talking about his interests in front of any friends and/or "females" of Adam or Jonny. Calling the Old Woman Out: After five seasons of his mother, Cynthia being horrible, Martin finally calls her out on it in "Dad’s Birthday", though only briefly. Catchphrase: "Hello bambinos", "Shit on it!", and "It's boiling", which are often said in every episode. "Any females?" comes up slightly less often. Cloudcuckoolander: He's a strange character who often seems to be in his own world.

characters.name: Adam "Pusface" Goodman
characters.description: Catchphrase: Has a habit of saying "Brilliant" or "Fantastic" in a sarcastic manner, not unlike WillMcKenziefromThe Inbetweeners, Simon Birds' most famous role. Also, "Alright Pissface!" toward Jonny. Deadpan Snarker: Easily the snarkiest person in the family; almost any time he speaks to someone, a snarky deadpan comment will often follow. Expy: Personality-wise, Adam is basically Will fromThe Inbetweenersfive years later. Manchild: Alongside his brother, Adam occasionally acts immature usually when the brothers prank each other or when there is an unwelcome guest at their dinner. The episodeThe Pyjamastakes this tropeup to elevenwhen he stays at his parents' for a week and immediately reverts to acting like a spoilt child. Parental Favoritism: It has been strongly hinted throughout the series that Adam is clearly the favourite son.

characters.name: Jonny "Pissface" Goodman
characters.description: Catchphrase: "Alright Pusface!" toward Adam. Deadpan Snarker: Frequently trades blows with Adam in this regard, though in Jonny's case, it's most used when frustrated at someone's antics. Once an Episode: Jonny frequently takes some squirty cream out of the cupboard to put in his mouth (see above), and Jackie almost always tells him off for it. Sibling Rivalry: With Adam, and frequently tries to pull pranks on him, like putting salt in his water or giving him spaghetti as a christmas present. Sitcom Character Archetypes: Since he's a bit more brash than his older brother, he's The Bully.

characters.name: Cynthia "Horrible Grandma" Goodman
characters.description: Abusive Parents: Anytime Cynthia is on-screen, she can’t go a single appearance without mistreating her son Martin, even admitting to have found him "bearable" before he met Jackie. Embarrassing Nickname: Cynthia is known as "Horrible Grandma" because she's...well.. horrible to everyone, often picking apart everything she see's for no reason other than because she can. Even her own son, Martin, isn't good enough for her.In "Dad's Birthday", she mentions that she is quite aware of this nickname, though as they all point out, her behaviour is the reason why, and she's never tried to change it.. Hate Sink: Cynthia is called "Horrible Grandma", and is loathed by everyone. She insults their looks, deliberately damages their things, and keeps pointing out flaws that either don't exist, or are exaggerated, such as Jackie making tea "badly" because it was too hot. Even as early as Series 2's "Christmas", the rest of the Goodman family say they're waiting for her death.They don't seem hugely bothered when it actually happens, as Martin's tears of sorrow very quickly turn into tears of joy. Karma Houdini: She manages to get out of trouble with the police after deliberately smashing a cabinet in a hairdresser's, and then forces Martin to pay the damages. Although perhaps it'sLaser-Guided Karmawhen shefinally dies of a sudden heart attack in series 6. Screw Politeness, I'm a Senior!: She's an old woman and is horrible to everyone she meets.

characters.name: Eleanor Rachel "Nellie" Buller
characters.description: Awful Wedded Life: Nellie wasalmostmarried to the horrible Mr Morris in the latter’s third appearance. Innocently Insensitive: Nellie often makes comments that don't sound insensitive to her, but noticeably do to her family and/or anyone else.

characters.name: Auntie Val
characters.description: Honorary Aunt: She is Jackie's best friend and not actually related to the Goodmans. Jewish Mother: Occasionally this trope when her children are mentioned; and she likes to try matchmaking Adam and Jonny with various Jewish girls she knows. Nervous Wreck: She is prone to anxiety and breaking down in fits of tears. Prone to Tears: She cries at the slightest thing, often leading Jackie to have to drop everything to comfort her. Slap-Slap-Kiss: She constantly fights with her husband Larry and then makes up again, until they finally get a divorce.

characters.name: Jim
characters.description: Animal Lover: Jim likes Wilson and other animals...but none of the animals he's come into contact with like him. Catchphrase: "Hello all!" and "Shalom!", the former is what he says when greeting the Goodmans, the latter when he tries to be respectful of Jewish culture. Character Development: At the beginning of the series, Jim is genuinely creepy, coming off as a stalker, but as the series progresses, hisStalker with a Crushtendencies are downplayed and made more innocent, and he becomes merely eccentric (most of the time, at least). In the later episodes, he will still try and kiss Jackie (the target of said crush), but usually in an awkwardNo Social Skillsway, more than anything. Cloudcuckoolander: Jim has a habit of throwing non-sequiturs into conversation. In one episode, he decides to pop round to their house to tell them that their doorbell is broken (he knows because there's a sign on it), and when his dog runs away, he panics "What if someone's raped him?" Drop-In Character: Jim serves at this, as he knocks on the door of the familyOnce an Episodefor increasingly specific and bizarre reasons.

characters.name: Wilson
characters.description: Animals Hate Him: He doesn’t seem to like Jim, his owner.

characters.name: Milson
characters.description: Animals Hate Him: Like Wilson, Milson doesn’t seem to like Jim. Replacement Goldfish: He gets her after Wilson dies and gives her almost the same name.

characters.name: Mr Morris
characters.description: Cloudcuckoolander: He is as mad as a hatter, and even in his second episode, his attempts to keep calm are fruitless. Grumpy Old Man: Boy, is he ever. Hated by All: Nobody really likes him, and even Grandma Nelly, who is the only one to tolerate his behaviour (and even she tries changing it to be more reasonable) eventually leaves him at the alterby pretending to have a heart attack. Let's You and Him Fight: In "Mr Morris" it was with Martin, and in "Mr Morris Returns" it was with Jim. Never My Fault: Blames everyone but himself for any bad luck that comes his way, such as blaming a bowling ball for being faulty when he misses the pins from less than a metre away!

characters.name: Emma
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in "The Girlfriend".

characters.name: Katie
characters.description: Blackmail: Katie discovers the nudes Emma’s sister has sent to Adam, and uses this knowledge to blackmail him into doing what she wants. One-Shot Character: Only appears in "The Girlfriend". Precocious Crush: Katie is 8 years old (9 in June!) and has a crush on Adam, an adult, and someone who used to babysit her years back. During her only appearance in "The Girlfriend", she blackmails Adam into spending time with her over Emma — unless he wants Emma to see her sister sending nude photos of herself to Adam on his phone.

characters.name: Sheila "Bitchface" Bloom
characters.description: One-Shot Character: Only appears in "The Mercedes". Running Gag: She won’t stop going on about her Mercedes.

characters.name: Tony
characters.description: Bitch in Sheep's Clothing:Despite his awful jokes and irritating blinking, he comes across as a caring and helpful bloke when he believes Martin’s lie about Jackie’s mother dying. Then after Tony left, the family discover some things missing from the house and Martin suddenly remembers that Tony was a thief that stole from everyone. One-Shot Character: Only appears in "The Two Tonys". One-Steve Limit: He is one of two Tonys that Martin knew back in University. To Martin’s dismay, he ends up inviting the Tony that he hates.
